title: Too Comfortable to Survive? The Panda Paradox
subtitle: 大熊猫是否因舒适而面临威胁

paragraphs.en: New research suggests that pandas may be at risk of dying out because they are too comfortable. Experts say too much happiness can stop the bears from searching for new mates. Environmentalists have long believed that building roads or homes near the bears may threaten their survival by reducing or fragmenting their natural habitats, The Times reported. But the new research suggests that a modest degree of discomfort and fragmentation may actually help preserve panda populations.
paragraphs.zh: 新研究表明，大熊猫可能因过于舒适而面临灭绝风险。专家称，过度安逸会阻止它们寻找新伴侣。据《泰晤士报》报道，环保人士长期认为，在熊猫栖息地附近修路建房会减少或割裂其自然栖息地，从而威胁其生存。但这项新研究表明，适度的不适与栖息地碎片化实际上可能有助于保护大熊猫种群。

paragraphs.en: The research was conducted by scientists from Michigan State University. It concluded that pandas fail to wander off in search of new mates if they find their habitat too comfortable, resulting in a lack of vital genetic diversity. For their study — outlined in a paper in the journal Conservation Biology — the team looked at genetic diversity and spread among a Chinese panda population. The ideal level of perfectly livable habitat was found to be only 80% of an area, with the remainder either too harsh or too affected by human activity.
paragraphs.zh: 该研究由密歇根州立大学的科学家进行。研究得出结论：如果栖息地过于舒适，大熊猫就不会外出寻找新伴侣，从而导致关键遗传多样性的缺失。在《保护生物学》期刊发表的论文中，研究团队考察了中国一个大熊猫种群的遗传多样性及分布情况。研究发现，完全宜居的栖息地理想比例仅为80%，其余部分要么过于严酷，要么受人类活动影响过大。

paragraphs.en: The experts concluded that pandas should ideally be happy enough to thrive, but not so content that they don't want to move around and find new mates. Their conclusions about what The Guardian described as this sweet spot are in line with the so-called Goldilocks principle: that there can be just the right amount of something. The concept has been applied to a wide range of disciplines, from developmental psychology to economics and engineering.
paragraphs.zh: 专家总结道，理想状态下大熊猫应快乐到足以繁衍生息，但又不至于安于现状而不愿迁徙寻找新伴侣。《卫报》将这一结论称为“最佳平衡点”，与所谓的“金发姑娘原则”相符——即事物存在恰到好处的量。这一概念已应用于发展心理学、经济学和工程学等广泛领域。

paragraphs.en: Claudio Sillero, a professor of conservation biology at Oxford University, told the newspaper that the new findings could have implications beyond panda conservation. 'Most large animals that eat meat live in increasingly fragmented landscapes,' said Sillero, who was not involved in the research. 'It may well be that the messy nature of their relationship with human efforts induces more animals to scatter or travel further, and might result in greater genetic connectivity and enhanced population persistence.' The most recent count of pandas found that there were more than 1,800 left in the wild, putting them on the list of vulnerable, but not endangered, species.
paragraphs.zh: 牛津大学保护生物学教授克劳迪奥·西莱罗未参与该研究，他告诉该报，新发现的影响可能超出大熊猫保护范畴。西莱罗说：“大多数大型食肉动物都生活在日益碎片化的环境中。它们与人类活动之间这种混乱的关系，很可能促使更多动物分散或迁徙得更远，从而可能带来更强的基因连通性和种群持久性。”最新统计显示，野生大熊猫数量已超过1800只，被列为易危而非濒危物种。

words.meaning: adj. 易受伤害的，易危的
